chunk_summary: Guidelines for micronutrient safeguards and iron timing in severe acute malnutrition (SAM) emphasize avoiding duplication of therapeutic micronutrients and delaying iron supplementation until the rehabilitation phase.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of avoiding micronutrient duplication in SAM care protocols.
Learn the timing and rationale for iron supplementation during SAM treatment phases.
Identify operational safeguards to prevent toxicity or missed supplementation in SAM management.
key_facts: Iron supplementation should not begin during the stabilization phase due to risks of oxidative stress and pathogen growth.
Iron is typically introduced after 7 days, once appetite improves and rehabilitation feeding is established.
Therapeutic foods and facility micronutrient mixes often contain low-iron or iron-free formulations during stabilization.
Vitamin A dosing must be documented separately to prevent repeat massive dosing.
Discharge planning should include a clear list of ongoing supplementation and follow-up dates.
reference_quote: Iron supplementation should not be started during the stabilization phase due to risks of oxidative stress and pathogen growth. (Source: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK154454/)
document_key_topics: Operational guidelines for therapeutic feeding in SAM
